title: PASTA: A Modular Program Analysis Tool Framework for Accelerators
authors: Mao Lin, Hyeran Jeon, Keren Zhou
abstract: The increasing complexity and diversity of hardware accelerators in modern computing systems demand flexible, low-overhead program analysis tools. We present PASTA, a low-overhead and modular Program AnalysiS Tool Framework for Accelerators. PASTA abstracts over low-level profiling APIs and diverse deep learning frameworks, offering users a unified interface to capture and analyze runtime events at multiple levels. Its extensible design enables researchers and practitioners to rapidly prototype custom tools with minimal overhead. We demonstrate the utility of PASTA by developing several analysis tools, including a deep learning workload characterization tool and a UVM optimization tool. Through extensive evaluation on mainstream deep learning workloads tested on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs under both single- and multi-GPU scenarios, we demonstrate PASTA's broad applicability. On NVIDIA GPUs, we further show that PASTA provides detailed performance insights with significantly lower overhead, up to 1.3*10^4 faster than conventional analysis tools, thanks to its GPU-accelerated backend. PASTA strikes a practical balance between usability, extensibility, and efficiency, making it well-suited for modern accelerator-based computing environments.
title_zh: PASTA：一种面向加速器的模块化程序分析工具框架
abstract_zh: 现代计算系统中硬件加速器的复杂性和多样性日益增加，迫切需要灵活且低开销的程序分析工具。本文提出了PASTA，一种低开销、模块化的加速器程序分析工具框架。PASTA 抽象了底层性能分析API和多种深度学习框架，为用户提供统一的接口，以在多个层次上捕获和分析运行时事件。其可扩展的设计使研究人员和开发者能够以极低的开销快速构建自定义分析工具。我们通过开发多个分析工具展示了PASTA的实用性，包括深度学习工作负载特征分析工具和统一虚拟内存（UVM）优化工具。在主流深度学习工作负载上的广泛评估表明，PASTA具有广泛的适用性，测试涵盖了NVIDIA和AMD GPU在单GPU和多GPU场景下的表现。在NVIDIA GPU上，得益于其GPU加速的后端，PASTA相比传统分析工具显著降低了开销，速度最高提升达1.3×10⁴倍，同时提供详细的性能洞察。PASTA在易用性、可扩展性和效率之间实现了良好的平衡，非常适合现代基于加速器的计算环境。

title: APFuzz: Towards Automatic Greybox Protocol Fuzzing
authors: Yu Wang, Yang Xiang, Chandra Thapa, Hajime Suzuki
abstract: Greybox protocol fuzzing is a random testing approach for stateful protocol implementations, where the input is protocol messages generated from mutations of seeds, and the search in the input space is driven by the feedback on coverage of both code and state. State model and message model are the core components of communication protocols, which also have significant impacts on protocol fuzzing. In this work, we propose APFuzz (Automatic greybox Protocol Fuzzer) with novel designs to increase the smartness of greybox protocol fuzzers from the perspectives of both the state model and the message model. On the one hand, APFuzz employs a two-stage process of static and dynamic analysis to automatically identify state variables, which are then used to infer an accurate state model during fuzzing. On the other hand, APFuzz introduces field-level mutation operations for binary protocols, leveraging message structure awareness enabled by Large Language Models. We conduct extensive experiments on a public protocol fuzzing benchmark, comparing APFuzz with the baseline fuzzer AFLNET as well as several state-of-the-art greybox protocol fuzzers.
title_zh: APFuzz：迈向自动化的灰盒协议模糊测试
abstract_zh: 灰盒协议模糊测试是一种针对有状态协议实现的随机测试方法，其输入是通过对种子进行变异生成的协议消息，而输入空间的搜索则由代码覆盖率和状态覆盖率的反馈信息驱动。状态模型和消息模型是通信协议的核心组成部分，也对协议模糊测试具有重要影响。在本研究中，我们提出了APFuzz（自动灰盒协议模糊测试工具），通过在状态模型和消息模型两个方面的创新设计，提升了灰盒协议模糊测试工具的智能化水平。一方面，APFuzz采用静态分析与动态分析相结合的两阶段方法，自动识别状态变量，并在模糊测试过程中利用这些变量推断出精确的状态模型。另一方面，APFuzz针对二进制协议引入了字段级的变异操作，利用大语言模型实现的消息结构感知能力来提升变异效率。我们在一个公开的协议模糊测试基准上进行了大量实验，将APFuzz与基线模糊测试工具AFLNET以及多个最先进的灰盒协议模糊测试工具进行了对比。

title: MulCovFuzz: A Multi-Component Coverage-Guided Greybox Fuzzer for 5G Protocol Testing
authors: Yu Wang, Yang Xiang, Chandra Thapa, Hajime Suzuki
abstract: As mobile networks transition to 5G infrastructure, ensuring robust security becomes more important due to the complex architecture and expanded attack surface. Traditional security testing approaches for 5G networks rely on black-box fuzzing techniques, which are limited by their inability to observe internal program state and coverage information. This paper presents MulCovFuzz, a novel coverage-guided greybox fuzzing tool for 5G network testing. Unlike existing tools that depend solely on system response, MulCovFuzz implements a multi-component coverage collection mechanism that dynamically monitors code coverage across different components of the 5G system architecture. Our approach introduces a novel testing paradigm that includes a scoring function combining coverage rewards with efficiency metrics to guide test case generation. We evaluate MulCovFuzz on open-source 5G implementation OpenAirInterface. Our experimental results demonstrate that MulCovFuzz significantly outperforms traditional fuzzing approaches, achieving a 5.85\% increase in branch coverage, 7.17\% increase in line coverage, and 16\% improvement in unique crash discovery during 24h fuzzing testing. MulCovFuzz uncovered three zero-day vulnerabilities, two of which were not identified by any other fuzzing technique. This work contributes to the advancement of security testing tools for next-generation mobile networks.
title_zh: MulCovFuzz：一种面向5G协议测试的多组件覆盖率引导的灰盒模糊测试工具
abstract_zh: 随着移动网络向5G基础设施演进，由于其架构更加复杂且攻击面扩大，确保强大的安全性变得愈发重要。传统的5G网络安全测试方法依赖黑盒模糊测试技术，这些方法受限于无法观察程序内部状态和覆盖率信息。本文提出MulCovFuzz，一种面向5G网络测试的新型基于覆盖率引导的灰盒模糊测试工具。与仅依赖系统响应的现有工具不同，MulCovFuzz实现了一种多组件覆盖率收集机制，能够动态监控5G系统架构中不同组件的代码覆盖率。我们的方法引入了一种新颖的测试范式，包含一个结合覆盖率奖励与效率指标的评分函数，以指导测试用例的生成。我们在开源5G实现OpenAirInterface上对MulCovFuzz进行了评估。实验结果表明，MulCovFuzz显著优于传统模糊测试方法，在24小时的测试中，分支覆盖率提高了5.85%，行覆盖率提高了7.17%，唯一崩溃发现率提升了16%。MulCovFuzz发现了三个零日漏洞，其中两个未被其他任何模糊测试技术检测到。本研究推动了下一代移动网络安全测试工具的发展。

title: Enhancing LLM-Based Test Generation by Eliminating Covered Code
authors: WeiZhe Xu, Mengyu Liu, Fanxin Kong
abstract: Automated test generation is essential for software quality assurance, with coverage rate serving as a key metric to ensure thorough testing. Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise in improving test generation, particularly in achieving higher coverage. However, while existing LLM-based test generation solutions perform well on small, isolated code snippets, they struggle when applied to complex methods under test. To address these issues, we propose a scalable LLM-based unit test generation method. Our approach consists of two key steps. The first step is context information retrieval, which uses both LLMs and static analysis to gather relevant contextual information associated with the complex methods under test. The second step, iterative test generation with code elimination, repeatedly generates unit tests for the code slice, tracks the achieved coverage, and selectively removes code segments that have already been covered. This process simplifies the testing task and mitigates issues arising from token limits or reduced reasoning effectiveness associated with excessively long contexts. Through comprehensive evaluations on open-source projects, our approach outperforms state-of-the-art LLM-based and search-based methods, demonstrating its effectiveness in achieving high coverage on complex methods.
title_zh: 通过消除已覆盖的代码来增强基于大语言模型的测试生成
abstract_zh: 自动化测试生成对于软件质量保证至关重要，而覆盖率是确保测试充分性的关键指标。近年来，大语言模型（LLM）在提升测试生成效果方面展现出巨大潜力，尤其在提高覆盖率方面表现突出。然而，现有的基于LLM的测试生成方法虽然在小型、孤立的代码片段上表现良好，但在面对复杂的被测方法时仍存在明显不足。为解决这些问题，我们提出了一种可扩展的基于LLM的单元测试生成方法。该方法包含两个关键步骤：第一步是上下文信息检索，结合使用LLM与静态分析技术，收集与复杂被测方法相关的上下文信息；第二步是基于代码消除的迭代测试生成，即对代码片段反复生成单元测试，跟踪已实现的覆盖率，并有选择性地移除已被覆盖的代码部分。该过程简化了测试任务，缓解了因上下文过长导致的令牌限制或推理能力下降等问题。通过对多个开源项目的综合评估，我们的方法在复杂方法上的覆盖率表现优于当前最先进的基于LLM和基于搜索的测试生成方法，验证了其有效性。

title: Modular Top Level Architecture Synthesis and Signal Routing in a 32-Bit RISC Processor
authors: Parinith M, Manjunath G. Asuti, Sudarshan, Rekha. P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The RISC-V instruction set architecture (ISA) is gaining wide adoption in domains such as IoT, embedded systems, and academic research due to its open-source, modular, and flexible design. A CPU core that is both efficient and easily verified is a basic prerequisite for such applications. In this work, we present the design and implementation of a Verilog HDL-based 32-bit single-cycle RISC-V processor that supports the RV32I instruction set. The processor integrates an Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), Register File, Control Unit, and Memory into a structured and modular datapath. Simulation and functional verification were performed using Eda Playground, where the correctness of instruction execution was confirmed through waveform analysis. The result of ALU operations is governed by the opcode and operands, where arithmetic operations (e.g., addition) perform A+B, logical operations (e.g., AND) perform <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A} \wedge \mathrm{B}$</tex>, and memory instructions handle load/store between registers and memory. Status flags such as zero, carry, negative, and overflow are updated accordingly. The proposed design demonstrates improved efficiency through optimized control logic and reduced critical path delay, ensuring faster instruction execution in single-cycle operation. This processor serves as a foundational model for future enhancements such as pipelined or low-power architectures aimed at increasing performance and energy efficiency. The proposed modular top-level architectural synthesis approach improves scalability, verification simplicity, and enables seamless extension toward pipelined RISC-V architectures.
title_zh: 32位RISC处理器的模块化顶层架构综合与信号布线
abstract_zh: RISC-V指令集架构（ISA）由于其开源、模块化和灵活的设计，正在物联网、嵌入式系统和学术研究等领域获得广泛采用。对于此类应用而言，一个高效且易于验证的CPU核心是基本前提。本文提出并实现了一种基于Verilog HDL的32位单周期RISC-V处理器，支持RV32I指令集。该处理器将算术逻辑单元（ALU）、寄存器文件、控制单元和存储器集成到一个结构清晰且模块化的数据通路中。使用Eda Playground进行了仿真和功能验证，并通过波形分析确认了指令执行的正确性。ALU操作的结果由操作码（opcode）和操作数决定：算术运算（如加法）执行A+B，逻辑运算（如与运算）执行$ \mathrm{A} \wedge \mathrm{B} $，而访存指令则负责在寄存器与存储器之间进行数据的加载和存储。零标志、进位标志、负数标志和溢出标志等状态标志也会相应更新。所提出的处理器设计通过优化控制逻辑和减少关键路径延迟，提升了执行效率，确保了单周期操作下的快速指令执行。该处理器可作为未来扩展的基础模型，例如向流水线结构或低功耗架构发展，以进一步提升性能和能效。本文提出的模块化顶层架构综合方法增强了设计的可扩展性，简化了验证过程，并为向流水线式RISC-V架构的平滑扩展提供了支持。

title: Design and Analysis of SIMD Functional Units for RISC-V P-Extension Instructions
authors: Nancy Gupta, Gopal Raut, David Selvakumar, Pranose J Edavoor, Prasoon, Nissy Niharika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: The RISC-V (RV) instruction set architecture (ISA) has gained widespread adoption due to its modularity, open source, and extensibility. The P-extension introduces SIMD (Single Instruction, Multiple Data) operations, which aim at efficient execution of neural networks, machine learning, computer vision, signal processing, and edge computing workloads. This paper presents the design, analysis, and verification of independent p-SIMD units with a hierarchical decoder for RV32 and RV64 P-extensions in Verilog HDL that support a comprehensive subset of 323 instructions. The front- end interface for the P-SIMD units facilitates integration with the existing RV32/64I core's pipeline at the execution stage. Functional simulation and UVM based verification have been performed in Vivado and QuestaSim to validate the accuracy and robustness. These P-SIMD units serve as the foundational component for high-performance SIMD / vector-capable RISC-V cores targeting compute-intensive embedded systems viz. edge computing, IoT etc.
title_zh: RISC-V P扩展指令的SIMD功能单元设计与分析
abstract_zh: RISC-V（RV）指令集架构（ISA）因其模块化、开源和可扩展性而得到广泛应用。P扩展引入了SIMD（单指令多数据）操作，旨在高效执行神经网络、机器学习、计算机视觉、信号处理和边缘计算等负载。本文介绍了针对RV32和RV64 P扩展，在Verilog HDL中设计、分析和验证的具有层次化解码器的独立p-SIMD单元，支持323条指令的完整子集。p-SIMD单元的前端接口便于在执行阶段与现有的RV32/64I核心流水线集成。通过Vivado和QuestaSim进行了功能仿真和基于UVM的验证，以确保其准确性和鲁棒性。这些p-SIMD单元作为高性能SIMD/向量型RISC-V核心的基础组件，面向计算密集型嵌入式系统，如边缘计算、物联网等应用。

title: StabilEdge, Formal Verification of Edge AI Applications
authors: Luca Lazzaroni, Riccardo Berta, Vafali Soltanmuradov, David Martín Gómez, Francesco Bellotti
abstract: This paper addresses the critical need for AI trustworthiness in smart IoT systems by focusing on the stability of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) in Edge AI. State-of-the-art model checkers guarantee feasibility of a single stability analysis. However, a comprehensive assessment requires multiple runs, considering different noise levels and output targets. This is feasible in Edge AI applications, because of the limited size of the involved DNNs. However, direct user interaction with the model checker via its command-line interface is suboptimal, with a high potential for error and inefficiency. We address this issue by proposing a higher-level tool, namely StabilEdge, that abstracts and automatically manages the technical complexities of the underlying verification engine through a graphical layer. The goal is to help designers to concentrate on application-level logic and constraints, rather than the underlying mechanics of the verification tool. StabilEdge performs parametric stability analysis by modeling realistic input perturbations, such as sensor noise/attacks, to assess if they may cause dangerous mispredictions. StabilEdge operationalizes a novel methodology unifying four complementary Edge-AI stability analyses: local robustness, focused evaluation of specific input features (sensitivity) and output target adherence (targeted robustness and sensitivity). The tool generates three key graphical outputs: stability constraint violations, class transition patterns, and input-output constraint trade-offs. It provides continuous verification feedback and specialized support for time series and vision tasks. Overall, StabilEdge automates the full verification workflow - from perturbation modeling to formal constraint generation and solver-level monitoring -, with no need for expert manual configuration. Evaluations on three edge AI tasks demonstrate that StabilEdge quantifies noise impact on DNN performance at a fine granularity, greatly enhancing the depth of NN performance assessment. This analysis yields also key insights, such as the explicit trade-off between model accuracy and its vulnerability to localized perturbations. We thus argue that the tool fulfills its goal of supporting designers in rigorously specifying DNN stability and making informed decisions on sensor selection and overall system architecture. The current implementation exploits the Marabou model checker and may integrate other engines in the future, especially to address scalability issues due to input/output and model size. To support free research in the field, StabilEdge is released open source at: https://github.com/Elios-Lab/StabilEdge.
title_zh: StabilEdge：边缘AI应用的形式化验证
abstract_zh:
本文针对智能物联网系统中人工智能可信度的关键需求，聚焦于边缘人工智能（Edge AI）中深度神经网络（DNN）的稳定性问题。当前最先进的模型检测工具能够保证单次稳定性分析的可行性。然而，全面的评估需要进行多次运行，考虑不同的噪声水平和输出目标。在边缘AI应用中，这种多轮分析是可行的，因为所涉及的DNN规模相对较小。然而，用户通过命令行接口直接与模型检测器交互的方式存在明显不足，容易出错且效率低下。为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种更高层次的工具——StabilEdge，该工具通过图形化界面，对底层验证引擎的技术复杂性进行抽象并实现自动化管理。其目标是帮助设计人员专注于应用层面的逻辑与约束，而非验证工具本身的底层机制。

StabilEdge通过建模现实中的输入扰动（如传感器噪声或攻击），进行参数化稳定性分析，以评估这些扰动是否可能导致危险的错误预测。该工具实现了一种创新的方法论，统一了四种互补的边缘AI稳定性分析：局部鲁棒性、特定输入特征的聚焦评估（敏感性）、以及对输出目标的遵循程度（定向鲁棒性和敏感性）。StabilEdge生成三种关键的图形化输出结果：稳定性约束违规情况、类别转移模式，以及输入-输出约束之间的权衡关系。它提供持续的验证反馈，并针对时间序列和视觉任务提供专门支持。

总体而言，StabilEdge实现了从扰动建模、形式化约束生成到求解器级别监控的全流程自动化验证，无需专家手动配置。在三个边缘AI任务上的评估表明，StabilEdge能够以细粒度量化噪声对DNN性能的影响，显著提升了神经网络性能评估的深度。该分析还揭示了关键洞察，例如模型准确性与其对局部扰动的脆弱性之间存在明确的权衡关系。因此，我们认为该工具成功实现了其目标，即支持设计人员严格定义DNN稳定性，并在传感器选择和整体系统架构方面做出更明智的决策。

当前版本的StabilEdge基于Marabou模型检测器实现，未来可集成其他验证引擎，特别是为应对因输入/输出维度和模型规模带来的可扩展性挑战。为支持本领域的开放研究，StabilEdge已作为开源项目发布，地址为：https://github.com/Elios-Lab/StabilEdge。

title: Low-Latency EDMA Framework for AI-Based DSP SoCs in Multimedia Real-Time Systems
authors: Monisha R, Niharika H, Sakshi Shivakumar, Vivek Raviraj D, Bindu S, Rekha P
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Real-time multimedia systems demand highspeed data transfer with minimal latency to support continuous video, audio, and image processing. Conventional CPU-based memory transfer introduces performance bottlenecks due to processor intervention in every transaction. To overcome this, the proposed work presents a low-latency Enhanced Direct Memory Access (EDMA) framework for AI-based DSP System-on-Chip (SoC) applications. The architecture automates memory transfers through dedicated hardware logic, thereby reducing CPU load and accelerating data movement. Both CPU and EDMA modules were implemented in RTL and synthesized using Cadence Genus in 45 nm technology, with functional verification carried out in Cadence SimVision. Results show that the EDMA design achieves lower cycle counts, efficient area utilization, and reduced power consumption compared to the CPU-only approach. Proposed EDMA achieves faster and more efficient data handling while reducing processor workload with total power consumption of 62.41nW and a chip area of 950.666 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> compared to the results reported in the literature. The framework demonstrates its potential for integration into highthroughput, real-time multimedia SoCs, ensuring faster data handling with minimal latency.
title_zh: 面向多媒体实时系统中基于AI的DSP SoC的低延迟EDMA框架
abstract_zh: 实时多媒体系统需要高速数据传输并具有极低的延迟，以支持连续的视频、音频和图像处理。传统的基于CPU的内存传输由于每次数据传输都需要处理器介入，导致了性能瓶颈。为解决这一问题，本文提出了一种面向基于人工智能的数字信号处理系统级芯片（AI-based DSP SoC）应用的低延迟增强型直接内存访问（EDMA）框架。该架构通过专用硬件逻辑实现内存传输的自动化，从而减轻CPU负载并加速数据移动。CPU和EDMA模块均以RTL实现，并采用Cadence Genus在45 nm工艺下完成综合，功能验证则通过Cadence SimVision完成。实验结果表明，与仅使用CPU的方法相比，所提出的EDMA设计实现了更少的时钟周期、更高的面积利用率以及更低的功耗。该EDMA方案在降低处理器工作负担的同时，实现了更快、更高效的数据处理，总功耗仅为62.41nW，芯片面积为950.666 μm²，优于现有文献报道的结果。该框架展现出在高吞吐量、实时多媒体SoC中集成应用的潜力，能够确保极低延迟下的快速数据处理。

title: Design and Verification of a Glitch-Filtered Clock Gating Controller Using System Verilog
authors: Shriniketana Kalale, Subodh Kumar Panda, Manjunath G Asuti, Sudarshan D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent and Innovative Technologies in Computing, Electrical and Electronics (IITCEE)
abstract: Dynamic Power reduction is a critical design aspect in modern FPGA based digital systems and SoC's. Clock gating is a widely implemented technique in reducing dynamic power consumption by disabling the clock toggles during inactive period of the system. However, conventional clock gating techniques do not offer a mechanism to filter the glitches which are present in the input signals. The proposed work offers a solution to overcome the glitches and optimizes the dynamic power consumption. The design is synthesized in the Cadence environment using 90 nm technology. The design is verified using a System Verilog based test-bench with randomized input pulses to validate the filtered signal and gated clock behaviour. The optimized area and dynamic power achieved are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128.556 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.68 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> respectively. This methodology can be effectively utilized in FPGA and SoC based designs to enhance energy efficiency and improve overall system reliability, making it highly suitable for complex digital applications.
title_zh: 基于System Verilog的抗毛刺时钟门控控制器设计与验证
abstract_zh: 在现代基于FPGA的数字系统和片上系统（SoC）设计中，动态功耗的降低是一个关键的设计要素。时钟门控是一种广泛应用的技术，通过在系统非活动期间禁用时钟翻转来减少动态功耗。然而，传统的时钟门控技术无法滤除输入信号中存在的毛刺。本文提出了一种能够克服毛刺问题并优化动态功耗的解决方案。该设计在Cadence环境中采用90 nm工艺进行综合，并使用基于System Verilog的测试平台进行验证，通过随机输入脉冲来验证滤波后的信号以及时钟门控行为。最终实现的优化面积和动态功耗分别为128.556 μm²和2.68 μW。该方法可有效应用于FPGA和SoC设计中，以提高能效并增强系统整体可靠性，非常适合复杂的数字应用。

title: Automated Software Test Case Generation Using Directional Partially Weighted Ensemble Large Language Models with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
authors: Wantana Sisomboon, Jakkrit Kaewyotha, Wararat Songpan
abstract: Ensuring software quality relies on testing practices that can reliably confirm whether delivered systems meet user requirements. While Acceptance Test-Driven Development (ATDD) encourages early collaboration between stakeholders and developers, creating comprehensive acceptance test cases remains a human task that heavily depends on domain expertise. Recent progress in Large Language Models (LLMs) has made automated test case generation feasible. However, approaches based on a single model often suffer from performance instability, uneven test coverage, and a limited ability to generalize across heterogeneous requirements. This study proposes an automated test case generation framework that combines directional partially weighted ensemble large language models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The framework assigns model influence dynamically according to directional performance trends and degrees of partial correctness and consolidates candidate outputs through a weighted majority-voting mechanism and cross-validation conducted over multiple folds. Five state-of-the-art LLMs, Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Qwen, and Mistral, were evaluated using 16 criteria derived from four widely adopted black box testing techniques: Boundary Value Analysis, Equivalence Partitioning, Use Case testing, and State Transition testing. Under the evaluated experimental conditions, the proposed framework achieved 100% test coverage, indicating complete consistency with the defined gold-standard acceptance criteria. Cross-validation results further demonstrate improved robustness, stronger generalization to previously unseen requirements, and linear scalability, supporting practical deployment in real-world software development environments, and complexity analysis to confirm linear throughout growth (O(n)), which supports efficient deployment under increasing workloads.
title_zh: 基于检索增强生成（RAG）的方向性部分加权集成大语言模型的自动化软件测试用例生成
abstract_zh: 确保软件质量依赖于能够可靠确认交付系统是否满足用户需求的测试实践。尽管验收测试驱动开发（ATDD）鼓励利益相关者与开发人员之间的早期协作，但编写全面的验收测试用例仍是一项高度依赖领域专业知识的人工任务。近年来，大语言模型（LLMs）的发展使得自动化测试用例生成成为可能。然而，基于单一模型的方法通常存在性能不稳定、测试覆盖不均衡以及在异构需求之间泛化能力有限等问题。本研究提出了一种结合方向性部分加权集成大语言模型（LLMs）与检索增强生成（RAG）的自动化测试用例生成框架。该框架根据模型在特定方向上的性能趋势和部分正确性程度动态分配模型影响力，并通过加权多数投票机制和多轮交叉验证整合候选输出。研究评估了五种最先进的大语言模型——Claude、ChatGPT、Gemini、Qwen 和 Mistral，评估依据来自四种广泛采用的黑盒测试技术（边界值分析、等价类划分、用例测试和状态转换测试）所导出的16项标准。在所设定的实验条件下，所提出的框架实现了100%的测试覆盖率，表明其与定义的黄金标准验收准则完全一致。交叉验证结果进一步表明，该框架具有更强的鲁棒性、对先前未见过的需求表现出更优的泛化能力，并具备线性可扩展性，能够在实际软件开发环境中有效部署。复杂度分析也证实了系统吞吐量呈线性增长（O(n)），支持在工作负载增加情况下的高效运行。
